# CS2 CFG Configurator

> A modular desktop application (Python / PySide6) for creating, editing, and managing Counter-Strike 2 (CS2) configuration files. Think of it as a VSCode-inspired GUI editor tailored specifically for CS2 cfg/autoexec files.

## Project Overview

- **Repository:** https://github.com/vsvito420/cs2-cfg-configurator
- **Author:** vsvito420 (Friedrichshafen, Germany)
- **License:** CC BY-NC 4.0 (non-commercial use)
- **Language:** Python 3.11+
- **UI Framework:** PySide6 (Qt6)
- **Theme:** Catppuccin Mocha
- **Platform:** Windows (primary), Browser Demo available
- **Status:** Work In Progress

## What This Project Does

CS2 CFG Configurator is a GUI tool that allows CS2 players to visually edit their game configuration files instead of manually editing raw `.cfg` text files. It supports:

- **CFG Editor:** Visual editor for CS2 console variables (cvars). Commands are sourced from structured JSON files per category (crosshair, viewmodel, networking, performance, video, map, buy-binds).
- **Bind Manager:** Create and manage keyboard binds — simple binds, toggle binds (A↔B), hold binds (+alias/-alias), and cfg-exec binds.
- **Buy Binds:** Visual editor for weapon purchase key bindings.
- **Settings Page:** App-level appearance settings (colors, sidebar width) applied live.

## Architecture

The project is **modular by design** — each feature lives in its own folder under `app/modules/`. Adding a new module only requires: creating a `QWidget` subclass, registering it in `main_window.py`, and adding an entry to `sidebar.py`.

```
cs2-cfg-configurator/
├── main.py                      # Entry point
├── requirements.txt
├── configs/                     # Generated & saved CFG files
├── data/                        # CS2 command definitions (JSON per category)
│   ├── crosshair/commands.json
│   ├── viewmodel/commands.json
│   ├── networking/commands.json
│   ├── performance/commands.json
│   ├── video/commands.json
│   ├── map/commands.json
│   └── buy-binds/commands.json
└── app/
    ├── main_window.py
    ├── sidebar.py
    ├── settings_manager.py
    └── modules/
        ├── cfg_editor/
        ├── bind_switcher/
        ├── buy_binds/
        └── settings_page/
```

## Key Files

- [README.md](https://github.com/vsvito420/cs2-cfg-configurator/blob/main/README.md): Full project documentation in German/English
- [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/vsvito420/cs2-cfg-configurator/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md): How to contribute and add modules
- [docs/SECURITY.md](https://github.com/vsvito420/cs2-cfg-configurator/blob/main/docs/SECURITY.md): Security analysis (addresses Windows Defender false-positives on the EXE)

## Quick Start

```bash
git clone https://github.com/vsvito420/cs2-cfg-configurator.git
cd cs2-cfg-configurator/cs2-cfg-configurator
pip install -r requirements.txt
python main.py
```

Or download the pre-built Windows EXE from [Releases](https://github.com/vsvito420/cs2-cfg-configurator/releases/latest).

## Web Demo

A browser-based demo is available at: https://vsvito420.github.io/cs2-cfg-configurator/docs/demo/demo.html  
Note: The web demo has limited functionality — no Steam/userdata folder access, no auto-detection of CS2 install path.

## Topics / Tags

counter-strike, counterstrike2, cs2, cfg, config, autoexec, keybinds, buy-binds, python, pyside6, qt6, desktop-app, modular, gui, game-config-editor
